title: Module: evidence hierarchy & levels of evidence
context: Understanding the evidence hierarchy — from case reports and expert opinion through to systematic reviews and meta-analyses — is fundamental to any appraisal skill. Patient advocates need to know why a phase-III RCT carries more weight than a single-arm study, and why a systematic review can be more or less reliable than any individual trial it synthesises. This module is the first in the M1 core curriculum, depends on M0 being green, and is itself patient-facing high-risk content.
objective: Author an original, plain-language educational module on the evidence hierarchy and levels of evidence, explaining how different study designs rank, why they rank that way, and what caveats apply — cited to authoritative sources and carrying dual high-tier sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers the evidence hierarchy from expert opinion and case reports through RCTs to systematic reviews and meta-analyses; explains the rationale for the hierarchy (susceptibility to bias, confounding control, replication).

Explains that position on the hierarchy is necessary but not sufficient: a poorly conducted RCT can be less reliable than a well-designed observational study; encourages critical appraisal rather than hierarchy-as-shortcut.
Cites GRADE, Cochrane, and relevant FDA/EMA guidance as authoritative sources; all citations resolve to sources.yaml entries with compatible allowedUse.
Written in plain language; no unexplained jargon without a glossary anchor.
Carries a persistent 'education only — not medical advice' notice and passes the advice-language checklist (task 002).
Passes the licence + provenance build gate and CI (build, lint, readability check).
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist (recorded in the review ledger): clinical framing is correct and nothing reads as treatment guidance.
Signed off by an experienced patient advocate (recorded in the review ledger): content is usable, accessible, and does not stray into advice.
Signed off by a methodology reviewer: evidence-hierarchy and grading descriptions are methodologically accurate.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Goals — Goal 2; § Dependencies — Cochrane Handbook, GRADE Handbook)
output: A plain-language educational module (content/modules/evidence-hierarchy.md) committed to the repo, covering evidence hierarchy and levels of evidence, provenance-cited, CI-green, and carrying dual high-tier sign-off plus methodology review.
